intentional_test_features: Cellulitis on a chronically lymphoedematous limb: a new sharply-demarcated hot, red, tender patch with low-grade fever superimposed on years of massive non-pitting swelling - the AI Doctor must recognise infection in a compromised limb that can spread rapidly

Mimic discrimination: distinguish acute cellulitis from DVT (chronic non-pitting hyperkeratotic swelling with a demarcated hot plaque vs deep diffuse calf ache), chronic venous insufficiency (no varicosities/haemosiderin here), and contact dermatitis (fever/tenderness favour infection)
Lymphatic-limb vulnerability: lymphoedematous limbs are prone to recurrent rapidly-progressive cellulitis and need prompt antibiotics plus skin-care to prevent recurrence and sepsis
Telehealth limits: cellulitis is a clinical diagnosis needing in-person examination to confirm, exclude DVT, and safely prescribe antibiotics - with clear sepsis/spread safety-netting
Systemically-well vs septic: currently low-grade fever without rigors/toxicity (oral-antibiotic territory), but must escalate on spreading erythema, rigors, or systemic upset

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
